intro.title: Transferable Skills Finder
intro.subhead: Find the skills you already have — from any job, anywhere — and turn them into interview stories.
intro.outcome: You will get a skills inventory and 2 ready-to-build interview stories.

page.jobs_why: List any jobs or roles — including work overseas, a family business, volunteering, or caring for family. They all count.
page.skills_title: Which of these have you done?
page.skills_why: Pick everything that fits. Think across all the work you listed.

jobs.title_ph: e.g., Restaurant server, or family store helper
jobs.did_ph: e.g., Took orders, handled cash, trained new staff

star.situation: At "{job}": think of one time something needed to get done.
star.task: What needed to happen? Say it in one sentence.
star.action: What did you do? You used this skill: {skill}.
star.action_no_skill: What did you do? Name the skill you used.
star.result: What was the result? Add a number if you can — time saved, people helped, money made.

results.title: Your Skills
results.narrative_h: Here's what we heard about you
results.narrative_body: Every job teaches skills you carry to the next one — even work that didn't feel "official." Here is what you already bring.
results.jobs_lead: Every one of these built real skills.
results.skills_lead: Skills you can use in any job — and talk about in an interview.
results.skills_empty: Go back and pick a few skills — you have more than you think.
results.star_lead: Fill in the blanks out loud. This is the STAR method interviewers love: Situation, Task, Action, Result.
results.cta_body: Take your skills and stories to the Interview Skills workshop to practice telling them.
results.cta_link: Go to the Interview Skills workshop →
